Name: M2 Baseline
Noted Behavior: Proper baseline with 500k steps and correct warmup. Agent begins improving after ~50k steps once replay buffer is filled. Reward rises from -21 toward -18 to -15 range.

Name: Very Low LR
Noted Behavior: Half the standard learning rate. Weight updates are too small to accumulate meaningful change in 500k steps. Loss decreases very slowly.

Name: Aggressive LR
Noted Behavior: 5x higher learning rate causes rapid but unstable updates. Reward initially climbs faster than baseline but then oscillates significantly.

Name: Very Short Horizon
Noted Behavior: gamma=0.90 makes future rewards decay steeply. Pong rallies last 50-200+ steps so agent struggles to learn defensive positioning.

Name: Large Batch + Higher LR
Noted Behavior: Paired increase of batch size and learning rate. Larger batch produces lower-variance gradients; higher LR restores update speed.

Name: Tiny Batch
Noted Behavior: batch=16 produces extremely noisy gradients. Reward curve is highly erratic with large swings between episodes.

Name: Fast Epsilon + Big Buffer
Noted Behavior: Epsilon decays in first 25k steps. Agent locks into greedy policy very early before it has learned reliable strategies.

Name: Slow Epsilon Decay
Noted Behavior: Epsilon decays over 40% of training. Agent remains highly exploratory initially resulting in flat reward but better final learning.

Name: Early Learning Start
Noted Behavior: Learning starts immediately after 100 transitions. Early updates are very noisy so buffer is not diverse enough causing unstable early loss.

Name: M2 Best Combined
Noted Behavior: Combines all best findings: lr=2.5e-4 fast learning batch=128 stable gradients eps decay over 15% balanced exploration.
